_comment: Source of truth for tokenomic.org WAF custom rules. Apply via infra/cloudflare/apply.sh. All rules carry the 'tkn-managed:' description prefix so apply.sh can delete-and-recreate without touching dashboard-authored rules.

rules.description: tkn-managed: block known scraper user agents
rules.expression: (lower(http.user_agent) contains "semrushbot") or (lower(http.user_agent) contains "ahrefsbot") or (lower(http.user_agent) contains "mj12bot") or (lower(http.user_agent) contains "dotbot") or (lower(http.user_agent) contains "petalbot") or (lower(http.user_agent) contains "serpstatbot") or (lower(http.user_agent) contains "blexbot") or (lower(http.user_agent) contains "dataforseobot") or (lower(http.user_agent) contains "seekport") or (lower(http.user_agent) contains "barkrowler") or (lower(http.user_agent) contains "zoominfobot") or (lower(http.user_agent) contains "megaindex") or (lower(http.user_agent) eq "") or (http.user_agent matches "(?i)^(curl|wget|python-requests|go-http-client|libwww-perl|java/)")
rules._comment: Empty UA + common library defaults are blocked at the edge. Real curl-from-CI traffic comes from the workers/api-worker -> api.cloudflare.com path, which is outbound and never hits this rule.

response.content: Service unavailable in your region for legal compliance reasons.

rules._comment: Crimea (UA-43), Sevastopol (UA-40), so-called DNR/LNR. Country-level OFAC list (CU/IR/KP/SY) is enforced inside both Workers in workers/*/security.js as defense-in-depth.

rules.description: tkn-managed: challenge suspicious POSTs to siwe verify
rules.expression: (http.request.method eq "POST") and (http.request.uri.path eq "/api/siwe/verify") and ((not http.request.headers["content-type"][0] contains "application/json") or (http.request.body.size lt 64) or (http.request.body.size gt 8192))
rules._comment: Real SIWE verify bodies are 200-800 bytes of JSON. Anything outside that envelope is almost certainly a probe; managed challenge keeps the false-positive rate low while killing botnets.

response.body: {"error":"Too many SIWE verifications from this network. Try again later."}

rate_limits._comment: Per-ASN (not per-IP) so a flood from a botnet sharing carrier-grade NAT cannot hide behind shared IPs. 30 verifies/min/ASN = 1 verify every 2s — enough headroom for a coffee shop or campus to do a release demo, low enough to neutralise credential-stuffing-on-wallets attacks. Pairs with workers/api-worker/security.js#authRateLimitMiddleware which is per-IP at 5/min.
